perturbation.original_text: 3. The contract parties are obligated to preclude d isclosure of confidential information to third part ies. The parties will entrust confidential information solely to those em ployees, consultants and third party companies whi ch are concerned with the processing of the order and whic h are legally or contractually obligated to secrecy .
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The contract parties are obligated to preclude d isclosure of confidential information to third part ies. The parties will entrust confidential information solely to those em ployees, consultants and third party companies whi ch are concerned with the processing of the order, even if they are not legally or contractually obligated to secrecy .
perturbation.explanation: By stating confidential information can be shared with those not legally bound to secrecy, the contract weakens the protection of confidential information, contradicting standard confidentiality practices and legal expectations.
perturbation.law_explanation: 18 U.S. Code § 1832 criminalizes the misappropriation of trade secrets. By allowing disclosure to those not legally bound to secrecy, the modified text increases the risk of misappropriation and undermines the legal protections afforded to trade secrets. It can be used as evidence that the firm did not take reasonable measures to ensure secrecy.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 404 | Berkman Klein Center Skip to the main content Page Cannot Be Found Brave traveler, your journey on our site has brought you to an abrupt dead end. Perhaps you wish to explore one of these other options? About Us Get Involved Events Community Publications Projects & Tools Last updated Mar 27, 2024
perturbation.justification: The changed text states confidential information can be shared with those not legally bound to secrecy. The Law Explanation states this increases the risk of misappropriation and undermines the legal protections afforded to trade secrets, because it can be used as evidence that the firm did not take reasonable measures to ensure secrecy. Scraped Law Snippet 1 mentions 18 U.S.C. 1 note related to the Economic Espionage Act of 1996. Snippet 2 is not helpful. By allowing disclosure to those not legally bound to secrecy, the modified text directly contradicts the need to protect trade secrets as highlighted in the law explanation, making the legal obligation of confidentiality much weaker.

perturbation.original_text: 8. This agreement becomes effective with the signat ures of both parties and is valid for three years. It is tacitly extended by one year if it is not terminated three months before its expiration.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. This agreement becomes effective with the signat ures of both parties and is valid for three years. It is tacitly extended indefinitely if it is not terminated three months before its expiration.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the extension clause to 'indefinitely' creates ambiguity and potential conflict with laws governing contract duration and enforceability. Some jurisdictions may limit perpetual contracts.
perturbation.law_explanation: BGB § 307 deals with the unreasonableness of standard business terms. An indefinite extension of a contract may be considered unreasonable, especially if it disadvantages one party excessively. The original agreement is under German law, as stated in point 11. This modification may be deemed invalid under German law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 307 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 307 Inhaltskontrolle (1) Bestimmungen in Allgemeinen Geschäftsbedingungen sind unwirksam, wenn sie den Vertragspartner des Verwenders entgegen den Geboten von Treu und Glauben unangemessen benachteiligen. Eine unangemessene Benachteiligung kann sich auch daraus ergeben, dass die Bestimmung nicht klar und verständlich ist. (2) Eine unangemessene Benachteiligung ist im Zweifel anzunehmen, wenn eine Bestimmung 1. mit wesentlichen Grundgedanken der gesetzlichen Regelung, von der abgewichen wird, nicht zu vereinbaren ist oder 2. wesentliche Rechte oder Pflichten, die sich aus der Natur des Vertrags ergeben, so einschränkt, dass die Erreichung des Vertragszwecks gefährdet ist. (3) Die Absätze 1 und 2 sowie die §§ 308 und 309 gelten nur für Bestimmungen in Allgemeinen Geschäftsbedingungen, durch die von Rechtsvorschriften abweichende oder diese ergänzende Regelungen vereinbart werden. Andere Bestimmungen können nach Absatz 1 Satz 2 in Verbindung mit Absatz 1 Satz 1 unwirksam sein. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The modification changes the contract extension from a one-year renewal to an indefinite extension. The Law Explanation states that under BGB § 307, an indefinite extension of a contract may be considered unreasonable and therefore invalid under German law. Both scraped law snippets refer to § 307 BGB, stating that terms in standard business conditions are ineffective if they unreasonably disadvantage the contract partner contrary to good faith. An indefinite extension could be seen as such a disadvantage, particularly if one party is locked into the agreement perpetually. This directly contradicts the principles outlined in § 307 BGB, creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The legal venue for all disputes arising from this agre ement is the head office of the company Helukabel in Hemmingen.
perturbation.changed_text: The legal venue for any dispute arising from this agreement is to be determined by the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the venue selection to be determined solely by one party might be unenforceable due to fairness and jurisdictional issues. Many jurisdictions require mutual agreement or reasonable connection to the chosen forum.
perturbation.law_explanation: 9 U.S. Code § 2 requires that agreements to arbitrate be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract. A clause that unilaterally grants venue selection power to one party could be challenged as unconscionable or lacking mutuality, potentially invalidating the arbitration agreement and violating the FAA if arbitration is involved.

perturbation.justification: The modified contract text, which grants venue selection solely to the disclosing party, contradicts the principle of fairness and mutuality required for enforceable agreements. While the provided law snippet (9 U.S. Code § 2) primarily addresses arbitration agreements, the Law Explanation suggests this principle extends to venue selection clauses generally. The original text specified a fixed venue (company's head office), ensuring both parties knew and agreed upon the forum. The altered text removes this mutual agreement, potentially making the clause unenforceable, especially if deemed unconscionable. The law snippet states agreements must be 'valid, irrevocable, and enforceable'. Unilateral venue selection could be argued as lacking validity due to the power imbalance it creates, thus contradicting the law.
